etymology_and_history.root_language: Language**: From "revitalize" + "-ed" from "re-" + "vital" + "-ize" + "-ed" (to make vital again)
etymology_and_history.original_meaning: Meaning**: Make vital again; having renewed energized and revitalized renewed with energized presence
etymology_and_history.semantic_evolution: Evolution**: Make vital → renewed energized → contemporary revitalized renewed and revitalized energized with renewed presence
etymology_and_history.cultural_shifts: Shifts**: Make vital → renewed energized → revitalized renewed → energized understanding

contemporary_usage.primary_definition: Definition**: Having revitalized renewed and revitalized energized; experiencing renewed energized and revitalized revitalization with revitalized presence
contemporary_usage.register: Positive; indicates renewal and energized restoration with refreshed revitalization
